name: OhMyForm
skip_reason: GitHub repo (ohmyform/ohmyform) was archived by the owner on Oct 31, 2024 and is now read-only; last release was in March 2022. Maintainers explicitly recommend migrating to Formbricks instead. Project is abandoned/discontinued.

enrichment.short_description: Self-hosted form and API platform that turns JSON-driven form schemas into APIs, PDFs, and data workflows.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Form.io is a self-hosted form and API platform with drag-and-drop building, auto-generated APIs, PDF forms, and enterprise SSO.

features.title: Drag-and-drop form builder
features.description: Build multi-step forms with conditional logic through a visual, no-code builder.

features.title: Automatic API generation
features.description: Every form automatically becomes a RESTful API endpoint for submissions and data routing.

features.title: PDF forms
features.description: Convert forms to pixel-perfect PDFs or upload existing PDFs and turn them into dynamic, data-bound forms.

features.title: Embedded reporting
features.description: Build interactive data analytics and reports on top of submission data using MongoDB aggregation.

features.title: E-Sign+ digital signatures
features.description: Capture cryptographic signatures that are bound to the submission data for legal validity.

features.title: Multi-tenancy
features.description: Run isolated sub-projects from a single deployment, suited to SaaS and multi-client platforms.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: Control access with RBAC, SAML authentication, and audit logging across projects.

pros: Open-source core avoids hard vendor lock-in and allows full self-hosting
No per-submission, per-API-call, or per-end-user usage fees
Automatic API generation removes the need to hand-build backend endpoints for every form
Enterprise features like SAML SSO, multi-tenancy, and audit logging are built in
Used by large organizations including Accenture, Deloitte, and PepsiCo
cons: Enterprise pricing is configuration-based, starting at $330/month per project or server environment
Advanced modules (PDF server, reporting UI, accessibility compliance) are priced as separate add-ons
Setup and self-hosting require developer resources rather than a simple SaaS sign-up

faqs.question: Is Form.io open source?
faqs.answer: Yes. Form.io has an open-source core, including the formio.js SDK and the Universal Agent Gateway, both available on GitHub.

faqs.question: Can Form.io be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, Form.io offers a self-hosted starter configuration in addition to its SaaS offering.

faqs.question: How does Form.io pricing work?
faqs.answer: Pricing is configuration-based rather than usage-based, charging per project and per API/PDF server environment instead of per form, submission, or API call.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Form.io SaaS offers a 30-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Who uses Form.io?
faqs.answer: Form.io lists enterprise and government customers including Accenture, Booz Allen Hamilton, Deloitte, LexisNexis, and the State of Ohio.

use_cases.title: Enterprise application forms
use_cases.description: Standardize data collection and API routing across large organizations without building custom backend endpoints for every form.
use_cases.audience: Enterprise IT and application development teams

use_cases.title: Regulated document workflows
use_cases.description: Convert paper PDF forms into dynamic digital forms and capture legally binding e-signatures.

use_cases.title: Multi-tenant SaaS platforms
use_cases.description: Isolate form and data configurations per client using Form.io's multi-tenancy feature.

features: Unlimited forms, submissions, and API calls

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Form.io?
paragraphs: Form.io is a self-hosted, enterprise application composition platform for building forms, APIs, and data workflows from JSON schemas.

Beyond drag-and-drop form building, it automatically generates REST APIs from form schemas, converts forms to PDFs, and supports role-based access, SAML SSO, and multi-tenancy for enterprise deployments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Form.io is for
paragraphs: Form.io is aimed at enterprise development teams, government agencies, and SaaS builders who need self-hosted control over form data and are willing to work with configuration-based, per-project enterprise pricing.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source Typeform alternative with AI-assisted form generation, conditional logic, and 40+ integrations.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: HeyForm is an open-source form builder with AI form generation, conditional logic, templates, and 40+ integrations like Slack and Zapier.

features.title: AI-powered form generation
features.description: Generate a complete form in seconds from a prompt using built-in AI tools.

features.title: Conditional logic
features.description: Personalize the form flow for respondents based on their previous answers.

features.title: Multiple endings
features.description: Route respondents to different outcome or redirect screens based on their responses.

features.title: Pre-built templates
features.description: Start from templates across categories like HR, marketing, education, and support.

features.title: 40+ integrations
features.description: Connect forms to tools including Slack, Google Sheets, Zapier, Stripe, and Mailchimp.

features.title: Responsive design
features.description: Forms are optimized to display correctly across mobile, tablet, and desktop.

features.title: Self-hosting
features.description: Deploy HeyForm on your own infrastructure since the project is fully open source.

pros: Fully open source with a self-hostable codebase (8,886+ GitHub stars)
AI-assisted form generation speeds up form creation
40+ native integrations including Slack, Google Sheets, and Zapier
Bootstrapped, independently run project (EarlyBird) with no external investors
cons: Custom domains and branding removal require the Premium plan or higher
Team member seats are capped on lower tiers (3 on Premium, 10 on Business)
Full feature set is spread across four separate pricing tiers

faqs.question: Is HeyForm open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, HeyForm is fully open source and can be self-hosted.

faqs.question: Does HeyForm offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, HeyForm offers a 7-day free trial on its Basic, Premium, and Business plans.

faqs.question: What is the cheapest paid plan?
faqs.answer: The Basic plan starts at $15/month and includes unlimited forms, 5,000 monthly responses, and 10GB of storage.

faqs.question: Who maintains HeyForm?
faqs.answer: HeyForm is built and maintained by EarlyBird, a bootstrapped, independent software company.

faqs.question: Can I remove HeyForm branding?
faqs.answer: Branding removal is available starting on the Premium plan.

use_cases.title: Lead and survey forms
use_cases.description: Build conditional, mobile-responsive forms for lead capture and surveys without a developer.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted data collection
use_cases.description: Deploy HeyForm on private infrastructure to keep form response data fully in-house.
use_cases.audience: Teams with data-residency or privacy requirements

long_form_blocks.heading: What is HeyForm?
paragraphs: HeyForm is an open-source form builder positioned as a Typeform alternative, aimed at small businesses that want to keep full control of their form data.
It combines AI-assisted form generation with conditional logic, customizable themes, and over 40 integrations, and is available both as a hosted service and as a self-hosted deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who HeyForm is for
paragraphs: HeyForm suits small businesses and independent teams that want a lower-cost, self-hostable alternative to closed-source form builders, especially those who value data ownership.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, MIT-licensed drag-and-drop form builder for collecting data with conditionals and integrations.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Formpress is a free, open-source drag-and-drop form builder with smart conditionals, custom themes, and embeddable forms.

features.title: Drag-and-drop builder
features.description: Create forms with a WYSIWYG drag-and-drop interface.

features.title: Smart conditionals
features.description: Show or hide fields dynamically based on form conditionals.

features.title: Custom themes
features.description: Customize the look of forms with configurable themes.

features.title: Multiple field types
features.description: Build forms using a range of supported field types.

features.title: Embeddable and shareable forms
features.description: Share forms via a direct link or embed them on external websites.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connect forms to third-party services via built-in integrations.

pros: Fully open source under the MIT license
Source code hosted on GitLab with a substantial commit history (2,300+ commits since 2019)
No cost since it's self-hosted and free to use
Lightweight, fast-loading forms
cons: No published cloud-hosted plan or managed pricing option
Self-hosting is required to run the product
Limited public documentation on company backing or support options

faqs.question: Is Formpress free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Formpress is fully open source and free to self-host.

faqs.question: What license does Formpress use?
faqs.answer: Formpress is licensed under the MIT license.

faqs.question: Where is the source code hosted?
faqs.answer: Formpress's source code is hosted on GitLab under the FormPress organization.

faqs.question: Does Formpress support conditional logic?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers 'smart conditionals' to control which fields display based on form logic.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted data collection forms
use_cases.description: Deploy Formpress on your own servers to collect survey or contact data without third-party hosting.
use_cases.audience: Developers and teams needing full data control

use_cases.title: Embedded website forms
use_cases.description: Embed Formpress forms directly into existing websites for lead capture or feedback.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Formpress?
paragraphs: Formpress is an open-source form builder for web data collection and online surveys, built around a drag-and-drop WYSIWYG editor.
It is MIT-licensed and hosted on GitLab, with support for smart conditionals, custom themes, multiple field types, and shareable or embeddable forms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Formpress is for
paragraphs: Formpress fits developers and organizations that want a free, self-hosted form builder they can fully control and customize rather than relying on a paid SaaS platform.

enrichment.short_description: Free, GPL-licensed open-source form and survey builder, the rebrand of Framaforms after five years.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Yakforms is a free, open-source (GPL 2.0) form and survey builder that can be self-hosted or used in SaaS mode.

features.title: Drag-and-drop form builder
features.description: Create surveys and questionnaires with multiple field types using a visual builder.

features.title: Design themes
features.description: Choose from multiple design themes to customize the look of a form.

features.title: Multi-channel sharing
features.description: Share forms via link, email, social media, or embed them on a website.

features.title: Built-in analytics
features.description: View response analytics with data visualization and export results to Excel.

features.title: Form cloning
features.description: Clone existing forms so others can reuse and adapt them.

pros: Fully open source under the GPL 2.0 license
Can be self-hosted for full control over form data
Large-scale real-world usage: reported 825,000+ forms created and 18 million+ responses collected
Adopted by organizations including Framasoft and France's Ministry of Foreign Affairs
cons: Site and documentation are primarily in French
No public pricing is listed for the hosted SaaS mode
Smaller international community than mainstream form-builder competitors

faqs.question: Is Yakforms free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Yakforms is free and open source under the GPL 2.0 license, and can be self-hosted.

faqs.question: What was Yakforms called before?
faqs.answer: Yakforms is a rebrand of Framaforms after five years under that name.

faqs.question: Can Yakforms be used as a hosted SaaS?
faqs.answer: Yes, the site mentions a SaaS mode, though no public pricing is listed for it.

faqs.question: Who uses Yakforms?
faqs.answer: Adopters include Framasoft and France's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, among other organizations running their own instances.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted surveys
use_cases.description: Run survey and questionnaire collection on your own infrastructure instead of proprietary tools like Google Forms.

use_cases.title: Public sector and nonprofit forms
use_cases.description: Collect public-facing form submissions at scale, as demonstrated by government and nonprofit adopters.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Yakforms?
paragraphs: Yakforms is a free and open-source form and survey builder, rebranded from Framaforms after five years under that name.
It offers a drag-and-drop builder, multiple design themes, built-in analytics with Excel export, and can be self-hosted or accessed through a SaaS mode.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Yakforms is for
paragraphs: Yakforms is suited to organizations, particularly in French-speaking and public-sector contexts, that want a free alternative to Google Forms with the option to self-host.

name: TellForm
skip_reason: Project is no longer maintained; the maintainer stated the codebase 'is only intended for reference code' and cited hurdles to continuing development. Its own community created a successor project, OhMyForm, which has since also been archived. Site returned HTTP 403 on fetch attempts.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source JavaScript UI library suite for building custom survey, form, and dashboard tools with a one-time developer license.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SurveyJS offers open-source JavaScript libraries for forms, surveys, dashboards, and PDF generation, with a free tier and paid developer licenses.

features.title: Form Library
features.description: Render interactive, JSON-schema-driven forms and surveys in React, Angular, Vue 3, or vanilla JavaScript.

features.title: Survey Creator
features.description: A drag-and-drop builder that generates the underlying JSON configuration for a form or survey.

features.title: Dashboard
features.description: Visualize collected survey response data through charts and tables.

features.title: PDF Generator
features.description: Convert forms to editable or pre-filled PDF documents.

features.title: Accessibility compliance
features.description: Built to meet WCAG, Section 508, and ARIA accessibility standards.

features.title: Offline data collection
features.description: Collect form responses without an active internet connection.

features.title: AI-assisted form generation
features.description: Generate forms with the help of built-in AI assistance.

pros: Open-source core (Form Library) is MIT-licensed and free
Unlimited usage with no caps on forms, responses, admins, or submissions
Supports React, Angular, Vue 3, and vanilla JavaScript
Royalty-free developer licenses cover distribution without extra end-user fees
WCAG/Section 508/ARIA accessibility compliance built in
cons: Full commercial toolset (Creator, Dashboard, PDF Generator) requires a paid developer license starting at $569 for the first year
Enterprise tier pricing starts at $2,299 with custom quotes
Requires developer integration rather than being a standalone, no-code SaaS app

faqs.question: Is SurveyJS free?
faqs.answer: The Essential tier and the core Form Library are free and open source under the MIT license; the Survey Creator, Dashboard, and PDF Generator require a paid developer license.

faqs.question: How is SurveyJS licensed?
faqs.answer: SurveyJS uses one-time-purchase developer licenses that are royalty-free, with no extra fees for end users, instances, or apps.

faqs.question: What frameworks does SurveyJS support?
faqs.answer: SurveyJS supports React, Angular, Vue 3, and vanilla JavaScript.

faqs.question: What is included with a developer license?
faqs.answer: A license includes the first 12 months of maintenance; renewals extend continued support and access to new updates.

faqs.question: Does SurveyJS support offline use?
faqs.answer: Yes, SurveyJS supports offline data collection.

use_cases.title: Embedded survey tools
use_cases.description: Add form and survey creation, data collection, and analytics directly inside a web application.

use_cases.title: Accessible public-sector forms
use_cases.description: Build forms that meet WCAG/Section 508/ARIA accessibility requirements.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SurveyJS?
paragraphs: SurveyJS is a suite of JavaScript UI libraries for building custom survey, form, dashboard, and PDF-generation tools inside web applications.
The core Form Library is open source and MIT-licensed and free to use, while the Survey Creator, Dashboard, and PDF Generator are available under paid, royalty-free developer licenses.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SurveyJS is for
paragraphs: SurveyJS is built for software developers who want to embed survey and form functionality directly into their own React, Angular, Vue, or JavaScript applications rather than using a hosted SaaS form tool.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source form builder with unlimited free submissions, self-hosting via Docker, and a paid hosted tier.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OpnForm is an open-source form builder with unlimited free forms and submissions, self-hosting via Docker, and paid Pro, Business, and Enterprise plans.

features.title: Unlimited forms and submissions
features.description: Free plan includes unlimited forms and submissions with no per-response charges.

features.title: Multi-step and single-page layouts
features.description: Build forms as either a single page or a guided multi-step flow.

features.title: Conditional logic and computed fields
features.description: Show or hide fields dynamically and calculate values based on other responses.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connect forms to Slack, Discord, Google Sheets, Zapier, and Stripe.

features.title: Custom domains
features.description: Serve forms on a custom domain (available on paid plans).

features.title: Self-hosting
features.description: Self-host OpnForm via Docker as a free Community Edition or a licensed Enterprise edition.

features.title: SSO and audit logs
features.description: Enterprise plan adds SAML/LDAP SSO and audit logging.

pros: Free plan offers unlimited forms and submissions with no per-response charges
Fully open source (3,000+ GitHub stars) with self-hosting via Docker
EU hosting with GDPR-compliant architecture
Wide integration list including Slack, Discord, Google Sheets, Zapier, and Stripe
cons: Free plan requires OpnForm branding on forms
Advanced integrations like HubSpot and Salesforce are locked behind the $67/month Business plan
Self-hosted Enterprise license costs $1,999/year per instance

faqs.question: Is OpnForm free?
faqs.answer: Yes, OpnForm has a free plan with unlimited forms and submissions, plus a free, self-hostable Community Edition.

faqs.question: Can OpnForm be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, via Docker, either as the free Community Edition or a licensed Enterprise edition at $1,999/year per instance.

faqs.question: What does the Pro plan cost?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan starts at $25/month and removes branding while adding custom domains and more integrations.

faqs.question: Does OpnForm offer discounts for annual billing?
faqs.answer: Yes, all paid plans offer a 15% discount when billed yearly.

use_cases.title: Startup and small business forms
use_cases.description: Collect unlimited responses on the free plan without worrying about per-submission costs.

use_cases.title: Compliance-sensitive data collection
use_cases.description: Self-host or use EU-hosted infrastructure with GDPR-compliant handling for sensitive form data.
use_cases.audience: Teams in the EU or with data-residency requirements

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OpnForm?
paragraphs: OpnForm is an open-source form builder that lets users create forms, surveys, and workflows with unlimited response collection on its free tier.

It offers drag-and-drop building, conditional logic, computed fields, and integrations with tools like Slack, Google Sheets, and Zapier, and can be self-hosted via Docker in addition to its hosted plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OpnForm is for
paragraphs: OpnForm fits teams that want unlimited free form submissions or the option to self-host, ranging from small businesses to larger organizations needing SSO and audit logging on the Enterprise plan.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source expert system for building guided interviews that assemble documents, built on Python and YAML.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Docassemble is a free, open-source Python/YAML platform for guided interviews and document assembly, generating PDF, RTF, and DOCX output.

features.title: Guided interviews
features.description: Run web-based, question-and-answer interviews that adapt based on user responses.

features.title: Document assembly
features.description: Generate downloadable documents in PDF, RTF, or DOCX format from interview answers.

features.title: WYSIWYG template editing
features.description: Edit .docx and .pdf document templates directly.

features.title: E-signature capture
features.description: Capture touchscreen signatures within an interview.

features.title: SMS and email integration
features.description: Send and receive messages via SMS and email as part of an interview flow.

features.title: OCR support
features.description: Extract text from scanned documents using optical character recognition.

features.title: Multilingual interviews
features.description: Build interviews that support multiple languages.

features.title: API access
features.description: Interact with Docassemble programmatically via its API for application submissions and data storage.

pros: Completely free with no pricing tiers; code is MIT-licensed and docs are CC BY 3.0
Highly extensible using Python for custom logic
Supports document assembly, e-signatures, OCR, and SMS/email in one platform
Built for legal and government use cases but usable as a general-purpose platform
Active open-source project accepting community contributions on GitHub
cons: Requires programming knowledge (Python, YAML, Markdown) to build interviews
Requires self-hosting and server setup rather than a simple SaaS sign-up
Steeper learning curve than simple drag-and-drop form builders

faqs.question: Is Docassemble free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Docassemble is completely free and open source, with code under the MIT license and documentation under CC BY 3.0.

faqs.question: What formats can Docassemble generate?
faqs.answer: It can generate downloadable documents in PDF, RTF, or DOCX format.

faqs.question: Who created Docassemble?
faqs.answer: Docassemble was created by Jonathan Pyle, a lawyer and computer programmer, initially to automate legal practice.

faqs.question: What languages does Docassemble use?
faqs.answer: Docassemble interviews are built using Python, YAML, and Markdown.

faqs.question: Can Docassemble be used outside of legal work?
faqs.answer: Yes, although built to automate legal practice, it is designed as a general-purpose guided-interview platform applicable across other fields.

use_cases.title: Legal document automation
use_cases.description: Build guided interviews that generate legal documents such as pleadings or forms based on user answers.
use_cases.audience: Law firms and legal aid organizations

use_cases.title: Government application processing
use_cases.description: Guide applicants through eligibility questions and generate the resulting application documents.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Docassemble?
paragraphs: Docassemble is a free, open-source expert system for guided interviews and document assembly, built on Python, YAML, and Markdown.
It conducts web-based interviews and generates downloadable documents in PDF, RTF, or DOCX, with additional support for e-signatures, OCR, SMS/email, and API access.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Docassemble is for
paragraphs: Docassemble is built for developers, legal aid organizations, and government agencies that need to automate guided interviews and document generation and are comfortable working with Python and YAML.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source real-time web log analyzer with a terminal dashboard and browser-based reporting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: GoAccess is a free, open-source, MIT-licensed real-time log analyzer with a terminal and web dashboard for Apache, Nginx, and more.

features.title: Real-time analysis
features.description: Processes logs with millisecond/second updates in a terminal (TUI) or web dashboard, written in C for speed.

features.title: Broad log format support
features.description: Parses logs from Apache, Nginx, AWS S3, CloudFront, and nearly all major web log formats.

features.title: Terminal and web dashboards
features.description: View statistics in a terminal interface or a browser-based dashboard with customizable color schemes.

features.title: Minimal dependencies
features.description: Only requires ncurses to run, keeping the install footprint small.

features.title: Report exports
features.description: Generate HTML, JSON, and CSV reports from parsed log data.

features.title: Traffic and threat visibility
features.description: Helps identify traffic patterns and potential threats like brute-force attempts and bot activity.

pros: Free and open source under the MIT license
Extremely lightweight with minimal dependencies (just ncurses)
Real-time updates in both terminal and web dashboard views
Runs fully self-hosted/offline without sending data to a third party
Large open-source community (20,000+ GitHub stars)
cons: No official managed cloud/SaaS hosting option
Primarily a command-line-first tool, which may not suit non-technical users
Requires direct access to raw server log files to analyze them

faqs.question: Is GoAccess free?
faqs.answer: Yes, GoAccess is free and open source under the MIT license.

faqs.question: What log formats does GoAccess support?
faqs.answer: It supports nearly all major web log formats, including Apache, Nginx, AWS S3, and CloudFront.

faqs.question: Can GoAccess run in a browser?
faqs.answer: Yes, in addition to its terminal dashboard, GoAccess offers a web-based dashboard.

faqs.question: What are the system requirements?
faqs.answer: GoAccess has minimal dependencies, requiring only ncurses to run.

use_cases.title: Server traffic monitoring
use_cases.description: Analyze web server access logs in real time to understand traffic patterns.

use_cases.title: Security monitoring
use_cases.description: Detect suspicious activity such as brute-force attempts and bot traffic from raw log data.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is GoAccess?
paragraphs: GoAccess is a real-time, open-source web log analyzer that runs as a terminal application or a browser-based dashboard.
Written in C for speed, it parses logs from sources like Apache, Nginx, AWS S3, and CloudFront, and can export HTML, JSON, and CSV reports.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who GoAccess is for
paragraphs: GoAccess is built for system administrators and security teams who need fast, self-hosted visibility into web server traffic and log data without relying on a third-party analytics service.
